purpose: Test whether refreshing the existing 14-day momentum membership only once every three UTC days materially lowers turnover while preserving the parent signal path and cost-adjusted expectation.

selection_record.selection_reason: The frozen three-day cadence reduced target turnover 31.05% while retaining 84.35% of symbol-side slots versus the daily parent path before returns were opened.
selection_record.prohibited_reselection: The two-day and four-day variants remain sensitivity evidence only and cannot replace the frozen three-day primary after outcomes were viewed.

signal.source: strictly closed OKX 15-minute candles resampled to complete 4-hour bars
signal.universe: 18 mature OKX USDT perpetuals with complete funding history
signal.direction: long cross-sectional winners and short cross-sectional losers
signal.entry_price: next tradable 15-minute open after the completed 4-hour signal bar
signal.position_policy: carry the unchanged target between refresh dates; do not inspect interim ranks

historical_evidence.random_time_warning: Borderline pass: one minimum empirical count below the frozen 0.05 threshold; forward evidence is mandatory.

prohibited_actions: change the three-day cadence or calendar anchor after registration
select the better two-day or four-day neighbor after viewing outcomes
change the 14-day formation window, entry rank, exit rank, direction or universe
add volatility, funding, volume, market-state or symbol-specific filters
lower costs, funding reserves, drawdown limits or concentration limits
count any refresh with an entry before registration as prospective
promote the variant into formal A-grade signals, approved manifests or order paths automatically
